document.title: SocratexBusinessDev Workflow — how to do things

routing.purpose: Main 'how to do things' router for business dev work: classify the task, open the matching flow, apply the reminders. Router first, then flow implementations, then shared engineering capsules.
read_when: For every task under the SocratexBusinessDev work profile, as the operating router.
When PIPELINE-CONFIG.json selects SocratexBusinessDev as the work profile.
do_not_read_when: When a smaller current-state or subsystem capsule already answers the request.

router.title: 🧭 Router — if you do X, open flow Y

lifecycle.title: 🔁 Lifecycle — 8 phases for a ticketed task

flow_dev_logging.title: 📝 Flow — Dev-only logging for the agent

routing.purpose: Work model for legacy or unfamiliar business codebases — 8-phase task lifecycle with business storytelling, explicit unknown-unknown surfacing, user-owned lifecycle handoff.
read_when: When PIPELINE-CONFIG.json sets the active project profile reference to SocratexForeignLegacy.
<text 125 chars: When the task is foreign/legacy business code and the user expects business storytelling plus user-owned PR/ticket lifec...>
do_not_read_when: When the project is greenfield with agent-owned architecture.
When the project is game engine or simulation work — use SocratexGamedev profile instead.
When the default clean code-pack workflow is sufficient.

routing.purpose: Compact project workflow router, including task classification, read rules, approval behavior, finish checks, and contextual action rules.
read_when: When this document is explicitly referenced.
When workflow rules select this document for the current task.
do_not_read_when: When a smaller current-state or subsystem capsule is sufficient.
